message:
type: SURVEY
files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Adding trans tag metadata (note/created/modified string-literal params) tracking. Need to edit InternationalizationExtension.parse() (the while-loop that parses trans tag params, around lines 354-467) to detect metadata param names and collect them into a metadata dict, and _make_node() (lines 509-571) to add a metadata param and set output_node.trans_metadata when non-empty.

type: SURVEY
files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Adding domain param to trans tag: parse() domain='...' parsing/validation, _make_node() using dgettext/dngettext when domain set, _install_callables() exposing dgettext/dngettext globals

type: DRAFT
files: src/jinja2/ext.py
region: InternationalizationExtension.parse() method
code:     def parse(self, parser: "Parser") -> t.Union[nodes.Node, t.List[nodes.Node]]:
        """Parse a translatable tag."""
        lineno = next(parser.stream).lineno
        num_called_num = False

        # find all the variables referenced.  Additionally a variable can be
        # defined in the body of the trans block too, but this is checked at
        # a later state.
        plural_expr: t.Optional[nodes.Expr] = None
        plural_expr_assignment: t.Optional[nodes.Assign] = None
        variables: t.Dict[str, nodes.Expr] = {}
        trimmed = None
        domain: t.Optional[str] = None
        trans_metadata: t.Dict[str, str] = {}
        while parser.stream.current.type != "block_end":
            if variables:
                parser.stream.expect("comma")

            # skip colon for python compatibility
            if parser.stream.skip_if("colon"):
                break

            token = parser.stream.expect("name")
            if token.value in variables:
                parser.fail(
                    f"translatable variable {token.value!r} defined twice.",
                    token.lineno,
                    exc=TemplateAssertionError,
                )

            # expressions
            if parser.stream.current.type == "assign":
                next(parser.stream)
                value = parser.parse_expression()

                if token.value == "domain":
                    if domain is not None:
                        parser.fail(
                            "translation domain specified twice.",
                            token.lineno,
                            exc=TemplateAssertionError,
                        )
                    if not isinstance(value, nodes.Const) or not isinstance(
                        value.value, str
                    ):
                        parser.fail(
                            "domain must be a string literal constant.",
                            token.lineno,
                            exc=TemplateAssertionError,
                        )
                    domain = value.value
                    continue
                elif token.value in ("note", "created", "modified"):
                    if token.value in trans_metadata:
                        parser.fail(
                            f"translation {token.value} specified twice.",
                            token.lineno,
                            exc=TemplateAssertionError,
                        )
                    if not isinstance(value, nodes.Const) or not isinstance(
                        value.value, str
                    ):
                        parser.fail(
                            f"{token.value} must be a string literal constant.",
                            token.lineno,
                            exc=TemplateAssertionError,
                        )
                    trans_metadata[token.value] = value.value
                    continue

                variables[token.value] = var = value
            elif trimmed is None and token.value in ("trimmed", "notrimmed"):
                trimmed = token.value == "trimmed"
                continue
            else:
                variables[token.value] = var = nodes.Name(token.value, "load")

            if plural_expr is None:
                if isinstance(var, nodes.Call):
                    plural_expr = nodes.Name("_trans", "load")
                    variables[token.value] = plural_expr
                    plural_expr_assignment = nodes.Assign(
                        nodes.Name("_trans", "store"), var
                    )
                else:
                    plural_expr = var
                num_called_num = token.value == "num"

        parser.stream.expect("block_end")

        plural = None
        have_plural = False
        referenced = set()

        # now parse until endtrans or pluralize
        singular_names, singular = self._parse_block(parser, True)
        if singular_names:
            referenced.update(singular_names)
            if plural_expr is None:
                plural_expr = nodes.Name(singular_names[0], "load")
                num_called_num = singular_names[0] == "num"

        # if we have a pluralize block, we parse that too
        if parser.stream.current.test("name:pluralize"):
            have_plural = True
            next(parser.stream)
            if parser.stream.current.type != "block_end":
                token = parser.stream.expect("name")
                if token.value not in variables:
                    parser.fail(
                        f"unknown variable {token.value!r} for pluralization",
                        token.lineno,
                        exc=TemplateAssertionError,
                    )
                plural_expr = variables[token.value]
                num_called_num = token.value == "num"
            parser.stream.expect("block_end")
            plural_names, plural = self._parse_block(parser, False)
            next(parser.stream)
            referenced.update(plural_names)
        else:
            next(parser.stream)

        # register free names as simple name expressions
        for name in referenced:
            if name not in variables:
                variables[name] = nodes.Name(name, "load")

        if not have_plural:
            plural_expr = None
        elif plural_expr is None:
            parser.fail("pluralize without variables", lineno)

        if trimmed is None:
            trimmed = self.environment.policies["ext.i18n.trimmed"]
        if trimmed:
            singular = self._trim_whitespace(singular)
            if plural:
                plural = self._trim_whitespace(plural)

        node = self._make_node(
            singular,
            plural,
            variables,
            plural_expr,
            bool(referenced),
            num_called_num and have_plural,
            domain=domain,
            trans_metadata=trans_metadata or None,
        )
        node.set_lineno(lineno)
        if plural_expr_assignment is not None:
            return [plural_expr_assignment, node]
        else:
            return node
note: Merged version handling both domain= and note/created/modified= as special assign-style params that get validated as string-literal constants and pulled out of variables dict; passes domain and trans_metadata kwargs to _make_node. Please review/adjust for your metadata needs.

type: DRAFT
files: src/jinja2/ext.py
region: InternationalizationExtension._make_node() method
code:     def _make_node(
        self,
        singular: str,
        plural: t.Optional[str],
        variables: t.Dict[str, nodes.Expr],
        plural_expr: t.Optional[nodes.Expr],
        vars_referenced: bool,
        num_called_num: bool,
        domain: t.Optional[str] = None,
        trans_metadata: t.Optional[t.Dict[str, str]] = None,
    ) -> nodes.Output:
        """Generates a useful node from the data provided."""
        newstyle = self.environment.newstyle_gettext  # type: ignore
        node: nodes.Expr

        # no variables referenced?  no need to escape for old style
        # gettext invocations only if there are vars.
        if not vars_referenced and not newstyle:
            singular = singular.replace("%%", "%")
            if plural:
                plural = plural.replace("%%", "%")

        func_args: t.List[nodes.Expr] = []
        if domain is not None:
            func_args.append(nodes.Const(domain))

        # singular only:
        if plural_expr is None:
            func_name = "dgettext" if domain is not None else "gettext"
            gettext = nodes.Name(func_name, "load")
            node = nodes.Call(
                gettext, func_args + [nodes.Const(singular)], [], None, None
            )

        # singular and plural
        else:
            func_name = "dngettext" if domain is not None else "ngettext"
            ngettext = nodes.Name(func_name, "load")
            node = nodes.Call(
                ngettext,
                func_args + [nodes.Const(singular), nodes.Const(plural), plural_expr],
                [],
                None,
                None,
            )

        # in case newstyle gettext is used, the method is powerful
        # enough to handle the variable expansion and autoescape
        # handling itself
        if newstyle:
            for key, value in variables.items():
                # the function adds that later anyways in case num was
                # called num, so just skip it.
                if num_called_num and key == "num":
                    continue
                node.kwargs.append(nodes.Keyword(key, value))

        # otherwise do that here
        else:
            # mark the return value as safe if we are in an
            # environment with autoescaping turned on
            node = nodes.MarkSafeIfAutoescape(node)
            if variables:
                node = nodes.Mod(
                    node,
                    nodes.Dict(
                        [
                            nodes.Pair(nodes.Const(key), value)
                            for key, value in variables.items()
                        ]
                    ),
                )
        output_node = nodes.Output([node])
        if trans_metadata:
            output_node.trans_metadata = trans_metadata  # type: ignore
        return output_node
note: Adds domain (dgettext/dngettext) and trans_metadata (sets output_node.trans_metadata attr) support side by side. Adjust the trans_metadata attribute-setting/type if you had a different plan.

region: InternationalizationExtension.parse() method (full)
code:     def parse(self, parser: "Parser") -> t.Union[nodes.Node, t.List[nodes.Node]]:
        """Parse a translatable tag."""
        lineno = next(parser.stream).lineno
        num_called_num = False

        # find all the variables referenced.  Additionally a variable can be
        # defined in the body of the trans block too, but this is checked at
        # a later state.
        plural_expr: t.Optional[nodes.Expr] = None
        plural_expr_assignment: t.Optional[nodes.Assign] = None
        variables: t.Dict[str, nodes.Expr] = {}
        trimmed = None
        domain: t.Optional[str] = None
        metadata: t.Dict[str, str] = {}
        parsed_any = False
        while parser.stream.current.type != "block_end":
            if parsed_any:
                parser.stream.expect("comma")

            # skip colon for python compatibility
            if parser.stream.skip_if("colon"):
                break

            token = parser.stream.expect("name")
            parsed_any = True
            if token.value in variables:
                parser.fail(
                    f"translatable variable {token.value!r} defined twice.",
                    token.lineno,
                    exc=TemplateAssertionError,
                )

            if token.value in ("note", "created", "modified"):
                if parser.stream.current.type != "assign":
                    parser.fail(
                        f"metadata parameter {token.value!r} requires a value assignment.",
                        token.lineno,
                        exc=TemplateAssertionError,
                    )
                next(parser.stream)
                value = parser.parse_expression()
                if not (
                    isinstance(value, nodes.Const) and isinstance(value.value, str)
                ):
                    parser.fail(
                        f"metadata parameter {token.value!r} must be a string literal.",
                        token.lineno,
                        exc=TemplateAssertionError,
                    )
                metadata[token.value] = value.value
                continue
            elif token.value == "domain":
                if parser.stream.current.type != "assign":
                    parser.fail(
                        "domain parameter requires a value assignment.",
                        token.lineno,
                        exc=TemplateAssertionError,
                    )
                next(parser.stream)
                value = parser.parse_expression()
                if not (
                    isinstance(value, nodes.Const) and isinstance(value.value, str)
                ):
                    parser.fail(
                        "domain parameter must be a string literal.",
                        token.lineno,
                        exc=TemplateAssertionError,
                    )
                domain = value.value
                continue

            # expressions
            if parser.stream.current.type == "assign":
                next(parser.stream)
                variables[token.value] = var = parser.parse_expression()
            elif trimmed is None and token.value in ("trimmed", "notrimmed"):
                trimmed = token.value == "trimmed"
                continue
            else:
                variables[token.value] = var = nodes.Name(token.value, "load")

            if plural_expr is None:
                if isinstance(var, nodes.Call):
                    plural_expr = nodes.Name("_trans", "load")
                    variables[token.value] = plural_expr
                    plural_expr_assignment = nodes.Assign(
                        nodes.Name("_trans", "store"), var
                    )
                else:
                    plural_expr = var
                num_called_num = token.value == "num"

        parser.stream.expect("block_end")

        plural = None
        have_plural = False
        referenced = set()

        # now parse until endtrans or pluralize
        singular_names, singular = self._parse_block(parser, True)
        if singular_names:
            referenced.update(singular_names)
            if plural_expr is None:
                plural_expr = nodes.Name(singular_names[0], "load")
                num_called_num = singular_names[0] == "num"

        # if we have a pluralize block, we parse that too
        if parser.stream.current.test("name:pluralize"):
            have_plural = True
            next(parser.stream)
            if parser.stream.current.type != "block_end":
                token = parser.stream.expect("name")
                if token.value not in variables:
                    parser.fail(
                        f"unknown variable {token.value!r} for pluralization",
                        token.lineno,
                        exc=TemplateAssertionError,
                    )
                plural_expr = variables[token.value]
                num_called_num = token.value == "num"
            parser.stream.expect("block_end")
            plural_names, plural = self._parse_block(parser, False)
            next(parser.stream)
            referenced.update(plural_names)
        else:
            next(parser.stream)

        # register free names as simple name expressions
        for name in referenced:
            if name not in variables:
                variables[name] = nodes.Name(name, "load")

        if not have_plural:
            plural_expr = None
        elif plural_expr is None:
            parser.fail("pluralize without variables", lineno)

        if trimmed is None:
            trimmed = self.environment.policies["ext.i18n.trimmed"]
        if trimmed:
            singular = self._trim_whitespace(singular)
            if plural:
                plural = self._trim_whitespace(plural)

        node = self._make_node(
            singular,
            plural,
            variables,
            plural_expr,
            bool(referenced),
            num_called_num and have_plural,
            domain,
            metadata,
        )
        node.set_lineno(lineno)
        if plural_expr_assignment is not None:
            return [plural_expr_assignment, node]
        else:
            return node
note: Merged draft: handles note/created/modified metadata AND domain param together, plus fixed comma-parsing bug (parsed_any flag) so metadata/domain-only first tokens still require commas between subsequent params. Please review/adjust the domain-specific parts (error wording, whether domain allows non-string) to match your design, then send back your version.

        self,
        singular: str,

        variables: t.Dict[str, nodes.Expr],

    ) -> nodes.Output:

        func_name_plural = "ngettext" if domain is None else "dngettext"
        extra_args: t.List[nodes.Expr] = []

                gettext, extra_args + [nodes.Const(singular)], [], None, None

            node = nodes.Call(
                ngettext,
                extra_args + [nodes.Const(singular), nodes.Const(plural), plural_expr],
                [],
                None,
                None,
            )

            # environment with autoescaping turned on

                    node,

code:     def parse(self, parser: "Parser") -> t.Union[nodes.Node, t.List[nodes.Node]]:
        """Parse a translatable tag."""
        lineno = next(parser.stream).lineno
        num_called_num = False

        # find all the variables referenced.  Additionally a variable can be
        # defined in the body of the trans block too, but this is checked at
        # a later state.
        plural_expr: t.Optional[nodes.Expr] = None
        plural_expr_assignment: t.Optional[nodes.Assign] = None
        variables: t.Dict[str, nodes.Expr] = {}
        trimmed = None
        domain: t.Optional[str] = None
        metadata: t.Dict[str, str] = {}
        parsed_any = False
        while parser.stream.current.type != "block_end":
            if parsed_any:
                parser.stream.expect("comma")

            # skip colon for python compatibility
            if parser.stream.skip_if("colon"):
                break

            token = parser.stream.expect("name")
            parsed_any = True
            if token.value in variables:
                parser.fail(
                    f"translatable variable {token.value!r} defined twice.",
                    token.lineno,
                    exc=TemplateAssertionError,
                )

            if token.value in ("note", "created", "modified"):
                if parser.stream.current.type != "assign":
                    parser.fail(
                        f"metadata parameter {token.value!r} requires a value assignment.",
                        token.lineno,
                        exc=TemplateAssertionError,
                    )
                next(parser.stream)
                value = parser.parse_expression()
                if not (
                    isinstance(value, nodes.Const) and isinstance(value.value, str)
                ):
                    parser.fail(
                        f"metadata parameter {token.value!r} must be a string literal.",
                        token.lineno,
                        exc=TemplateAssertionError,
                    )
                metadata[token.value] = value.value
                continue
            elif token.value == "domain":
                if domain is not None:
                    parser.fail(
                        "domain parameter specified twice.",
                        token.lineno,
                        exc=TemplateAssertionError,
                    )
                if parser.stream.current.type != "assign":
                    parser.fail(
                        "domain parameter requires a value assignment.",
                        token.lineno,
                        exc=TemplateAssertionError,
                    )
                next(parser.stream)
                value = parser.parse_expression()
                if not (
                    isinstance(value, nodes.Const) and isinstance(value.value, str)
                ):
                    parser.fail(
                        "domain parameter must be a string literal.",
                        token.lineno,
                        exc=TemplateAssertionError,
                    )
                domain = value.value
                continue

            # expressions
            if parser.stream.current.type == "assign":
                next(parser.stream)
                variables[token.value] = var = parser.parse_expression()
            elif trimmed is None and token.value in ("trimmed", "notrimmed"):
                trimmed = token.value == "trimmed"
                continue
            else:
                variables[token.value] = var = nodes.Name(token.value, "load")

            if plural_expr is None:
                if isinstance(var, nodes.Call):
                    plural_expr = nodes.Name("_trans", "load")
                    variables[token.value] = plural_expr
                    plural_expr_assignment = nodes.Assign(
                        nodes.Name("_trans", "store"), var
                    )
                else:
                    plural_expr = var
                num_called_num = token.value == "num"

        parser.stream.expect("block_end")

        plural = None
        have_plural = False
        referenced = set()

        # now parse until endtrans or pluralize
        singular_names, singular = self._parse_block(parser, True)
        if singular_names:
            referenced.update(singular_names)
            if plural_expr is None:
                plural_expr = nodes.Name(singular_names[0], "load")
                num_called_num = singular_names[0] == "num"

        # if we have a pluralize block, we parse that too
        if parser.stream.current.test("name:pluralize"):
            have_plural = True
            next(parser.stream)
            if parser.stream.current.type != "block_end":
                token = parser.stream.expect("name")
                if token.value not in variables:
                    parser.fail(
                        f"unknown variable {token.value!r} for pluralization",
                        token.lineno,
                        exc=TemplateAssertionError,
                    )
                plural_expr = variables[token.value]
                num_called_num = token.value == "num"
            parser.stream.expect("block_end")
            plural_names, plural = self._parse_block(parser, False)
            next(parser.stream)
            referenced.update(plural_names)
        else:
            next(parser.stream)

        # register free names as simple name expressions
        for name in referenced:
            if name not in variables:
                variables[name] = nodes.Name(name, "load")

        if not have_plural:
            plural_expr = None
        elif plural_expr is None:
            parser.fail("pluralize without variables", lineno)

        if trimmed is None:
            trimmed = self.environment.policies["ext.i18n.trimmed"]
        if trimmed:
            singular = self._trim_whitespace(singular)
            if plural:
                plural = self._trim_whitespace(plural)

        node = self._make_node(
            singular,
            plural,
            variables,
            plural_expr,
            bool(referenced),
            num_called_num and have_plural,
            domain,
            metadata,
        )
        node.set_lineno(lineno)
        if plural_expr_assignment is not None:
            return [plural_expr_assignment, node]
        else:
            return node
note: Adopted your draft as-is, only added a duplicate-domain check (domain is not None -> fail with twice) mirroring your metadata style, needed since my domain error-handling spec requires a twice/duplicate error. No other changes.

type: AGREE
files: src/jinja2/ext.py
region: InternationalizationExtension._make_node() method
code:     def _make_node(
        self,
        singular: str,
        plural: t.Optional[str],
        variables: t.Dict[str, nodes.Expr],
        plural_expr: t.Optional[nodes.Expr],
        vars_referenced: bool,
        num_called_num: bool,
        domain: t.Optional[str] = None,
        metadata: t.Optional[t.Dict[str, str]] = None,
    ) -> nodes.Output:
        """Generates a useful node from the data provided."""
        newstyle = self.environment.newstyle_gettext  # type: ignore
        node: nodes.Expr

        # no variables referenced?  no need to escape for old style
        # gettext invocations only if there are vars.
        if not vars_referenced and not newstyle:
            singular = singular.replace("%%", "%")
            if plural:
                plural = plural.replace("%%", "%")

        func_name = "gettext" if domain is None else "dgettext"
        func_name_plural = "ngettext" if domain is None else "dngettext"
        extra_args: t.List[nodes.Expr] = []
        if domain is not None:
            extra_args.append(nodes.Const(domain))

        # singular only:
        if plural_expr is None:
            gettext = nodes.Name(func_name, "load")
            node = nodes.Call(
                gettext, extra_args + [nodes.Const(singular)], [], None, None
            )

        # singular and plural
        else:
            ngettext = nodes.Name(func_name_plural, "load")
            node = nodes.Call(
                ngettext,
                extra_args + [nodes.Const(singular), nodes.Const(plural), plural_expr],
                [],
                None,
                None,
            )

        # in case newstyle gettext is used, the method is powerful
        # enough to handle the variable expansion and autoescape
        # handling itself
        if newstyle:
            for key, value in variables.items():
                # the function adds that later anyways in case num was
                # called num, so just skip it.
                if num_called_num and key == "num":
                    continue
                node.kwargs.append(nodes.Keyword(key, value))

        # otherwise do that here
        else:
            # mark the return value as safe if we are in an
            # environment with autoescaping turned on
            node = nodes.MarkSafeIfAutoescape(node)
            if variables:
                node = nodes.Mod(
                    node,
                    nodes.Dict(
                        [
                            nodes.Pair(nodes.Const(key), value)
                            for key, value in variables.items()
                        ]
                    ),
                )

        output_node = nodes.Output([node])
        if metadata:
            output_node.trans_metadata = metadata
        return output_node

code:     def parse(self, parser: "Parser") -> t.Union[nodes.Node, t.List[nodes.Node]]:
        """Parse a translatable tag."""
        lineno = next(parser.stream).lineno
        num_called_num = False

        # find all the variables referenced.  Additionally a variable can be
        # defined in the body of the trans block too, but this is checked at
        # a later state.
        plural_expr: t.Optional[nodes.Expr] = None
        plural_expr_assignment: t.Optional[nodes.Assign] = None
        variables: t.Dict[str, nodes.Expr] = {}
        trimmed = None
        domain: t.Optional[str] = None
        trans_metadata: t.Dict[str, str] = {}
        while parser.stream.current.type != "block_end":
            if variables or trans_metadata or domain is not None:
                parser.stream.expect("comma")

            # skip colon for python compatibility
            if parser.stream.skip_if("colon"):
                break

            token = parser.stream.expect("name")
            if token.value in variables:
                parser.fail(
                    f"translatable variable {token.value!r} defined twice.",
                    token.lineno,
                    exc=TemplateAssertionError,
                )

            if (
                token.value in ("note", "created", "modified")
                and parser.stream.current.type != "assign"
            ):
                parser.fail(
                    f"metadata parameter {token.value!r} requires a value assignment.",
                    token.lineno,
                    exc=TemplateAssertionError,
                )

            # expressions
            if parser.stream.current.type == "assign":
                next(parser.stream)
                value = parser.parse_expression()

                if token.value == "domain":
                    if domain is not None:
                        parser.fail(
                            "translation domain specified twice.",
                            token.lineno,
                            exc=TemplateAssertionError,
                        )
                    if not isinstance(value, nodes.Const) or not isinstance(
                        value.value, str
                    ):
                        parser.fail(
                            "domain must be a string literal constant.",
                            token.lineno,
                            exc=TemplateAssertionError,
                        )
                    domain = value.value
                    continue
                elif token.value in ("note", "created", "modified"):
                    if token.value in trans_metadata:
                        parser.fail(
                            f"translation {token.value} specified twice.",
                            token.lineno,
                            exc=TemplateAssertionError,
                        )
                    if not isinstance(value, nodes.Const) or not isinstance(
                        value.value, str
                    ):
                        parser.fail(
                            f"metadata parameter {token.value!r} must be a string literal.",
                            token.lineno,
                            exc=TemplateAssertionError,
                        )
                    trans_metadata[token.value] = value.value
                    continue

                variables[token.value] = var = value
            elif trimmed is None and token.value in ("trimmed", "notrimmed"):
                trimmed = token.value == "trimmed"
                continue
            else:
                variables[token.value] = var = nodes.Name(token.value, "load")

            if plural_expr is None:
                if isinstance(var, nodes.Call):
                    plural_expr = nodes.Name("_trans", "load")
                    variables[token.value] = plural_expr
                    plural_expr_assignment = nodes.Assign(
                        nodes.Name("_trans", "store"), var
                    )
                else:
                    plural_expr = var
                num_called_num = token.value == "num"

        parser.stream.expect("block_end")

        plural = None
        have_plural = False
        referenced = set()

        # now parse until endtrans or pluralize
        singular_names, singular = self._parse_block(parser, True)
        if singular_names:
            referenced.update(singular_names)
            if plural_expr is None:
                plural_expr = nodes.Name(singular_names[0], "load")
                num_called_num = singular_names[0] == "num"

        # if we have a pluralize block, we parse that too
        if parser.stream.current.test("name:pluralize"):
            have_plural = True
            next(parser.stream)
            if parser.stream.current.type != "block_end":
                token = parser.stream.expect("name")
                if token.value not in variables:
                    parser.fail(
                        f"unknown variable {token.value!r} for pluralization",
                        token.lineno,
                        exc=TemplateAssertionError,
                    )
                plural_expr = variables[token.value]
                num_called_num = token.value == "num"
            parser.stream.expect("block_end")
            plural_names, plural = self._parse_block(parser, False)
            next(parser.stream)
            referenced.update(plural_names)
        else:
            next(parser.stream)

        # register free names as simple name expressions
        for name in referenced:
            if name not in variables:
                variables[name] = nodes.Name(name, "load")

        if not have_plural:
            plural_expr = None
        elif plural_expr is None:
            parser.fail("pluralize without variables", lineno)

        if trimmed is None:
            trimmed = self.environment.policies["ext.i18n.trimmed"]
        if trimmed:
            singular = self._trim_whitespace(singular)
            if plural:
                plural = self._trim_whitespace(plural)

        node = self._make_node(
            singular,
            plural,
            variables,
            plural_expr,
            bool(referenced),
            num_called_num and have_plural,
            domain=domain,
            trans_metadata=trans_metadata or None,
        )
        node.set_lineno(lineno)
        if plural_expr_assignment is not None:
            return [plural_expr_assignment, node]
        else:
            return node

IMPORTANT: don't blanket-fix this to 'parsed_any' -- that breaks test_ext.py::test_trimmed_vars which relies on 'trimmed x="world"' parsing with NO comma (trimmed intentionally never gates a comma, must stay that way). (2) added the missing 'metadata parameter {name!r} requires a value assignment' error for bare note/created/modified with no '='  -- your draft silently treated bare 'note' as a plain template variable. files: src/jinja2/ext.py
region: InternationalizationExtension._make_node() method
code:     def _make_node(
        self,
        singular: str,
        plural: t.Optional[str],
        variables: t.Dict[str, nodes.Expr],
        plural_expr: t.Optional[nodes.Expr],
        vars_referenced: bool,
        num_called_num: bool,
        domain: t.Optional[str] = None,
        metadata: t.Optional[t.Dict[str, str]] = None,
    ) -> nodes.Output:
        """Generates a useful node from the data provided."""
        newstyle = self.environment.newstyle_gettext  # type: ignore
        node: nodes.Expr

        # no variables referenced?  no need to escape for old style
        # gettext invocations only if there are vars.
        if not vars_referenced and not newstyle:
            singular = singular.replace("%%", "%")
            if plural:
                plural = plural.replace("%%", "%")

        func_name = "gettext" if domain is None else "dgettext"
        func_name_plural = "ngettext" if domain is None else "dngettext"
        extra_args: t.List[nodes.Expr] = []
        if domain is not None:
            extra_args.append(nodes.Const(domain))

        # singular only:
        if plural_expr is None:
            gettext = nodes.Name(func_name, "load")
            node = nodes.Call(
                gettext, extra_args + [nodes.Const(singular)], [], None, None
            )

        # singular and plural
        else:
            ngettext = nodes.Name(func_name_plural, "load")
            node = nodes.Call(
                ngettext,
                extra_args + [nodes.Const(singular), nodes.Const(plural), plural_expr],
                [],
                None,
                None,
            )

        # in case newstyle gettext is used, the method is powerful
        # enough to handle the variable expansion and autoescape
        # handling itself
        if newstyle:
            for key, value in variables.items():
                # the function adds that later anyways in case num was
                # called num, so just skip it.
                if num_called_num and key == "num":
                    continue
                node.kwargs.append(nodes.Keyword(key, value))

        # otherwise do that here
        else:
            # mark the return value as safe if we are in an
            # environment with autoescaping turned on
            node = nodes.MarkSafeIfAutoescape(node)
            if variables:
                node = nodes.Mod(
                    node,
                    nodes.Dict(
                        [
                            nodes.Pair(nodes.Const(key), value)
                            for key, value in variables.items()
                        ]
                    ),
                )

        output_node = nodes.Output([node])
        if metadata:
            output_node.trans_metadata = metadata
        return output_node

code:     def parse(self, parser: "Parser") -> t.Union[nodes.Node, t.List[nodes.Node]]:
        """Parse a translatable tag."""
        lineno = next(parser.stream).lineno
        num_called_num = False

        # find all the variables referenced.  Additionally a variable can be
        # defined in the body of the trans block too, but this is checked at
        # a later state.
        plural_expr: t.Optional[nodes.Expr] = None
        plural_expr_assignment: t.Optional[nodes.Assign] = None
        variables: t.Dict[str, nodes.Expr] = {}
        trimmed = None
        domain: t.Optional[str] = None

            if variables or metadata or domain is not None:

                break

            token = parser.stream.expect("name")
            if token.value in variables:
                parser.fail(
                    f"translatable variable {token.value!r} defined twice.",
                    token.lineno,
                    exc=TemplateAssertionError,
                )

            if token.value in ("note", "created", "modified"):
                if parser.stream.current.type != "assign":
                    parser.fail(
                        f"metadata parameter {token.value!r} requires a value assignment.",
                        token.lineno,
                        exc=TemplateAssertionError,
                    )
                next(parser.stream)
                value = parser.parse_expression()
                if not (
                    isinstance(value, nodes.Const) and isinstance(value.value, str)
                ):
                    parser.fail(
                        f"metadata parameter {token.value!r} must be a string literal.",
                        token.lineno,
                        exc=TemplateAssertionError,
                    )
                metadata[token.value] = value.value
                continue
            elif token.value == "domain":
                if domain is not None:
                    parser.fail(
                        "domain parameter specified twice.",
                        token.lineno,
                        exc=TemplateAssertionError,
                    )
                if parser.stream.current.type != "assign":
                    parser.fail(
                        "domain parameter requires a value assignment.",
                        token.lineno,
                        exc=TemplateAssertionError,
                    )
                next(parser.stream)
                value = parser.parse_expression()
                if not (
                    isinstance(value, nodes.Const) and isinstance(value.value, str)
                ):
                    parser.fail(
                        "domain parameter must be a string literal.",
                        token.lineno,
                        exc=TemplateAssertionError,
                    )
                domain = value.value
                continue

            # expressions
            if parser.stream.current.type == "assign":
                next(parser.stream)
                variables[token.value] = var = parser.parse_expression()
            elif trimmed is None and token.value in ("trimmed", "notrimmed"):
                trimmed = token.value == "trimmed"
                continue
            else:
                variables[token.value] = var = nodes.Name(token.value, "load")

            if plural_expr is None:
                if isinstance(var, nodes.Call):
                    plural_expr = nodes.Name("_trans", "load")
                    variables[token.value] = plural_expr
                    plural_expr_assignment = nodes.Assign(

                else:

                num_called_num = token.value == "num"

        parser.stream.expect("block_end")

        plural = None
        have_plural = False
        referenced = set()

        # now parse until endtrans or pluralize
        singular_names, singular = self._parse_block(parser, True)
        if singular_names:
            referenced.update(singular_names)
            if plural_expr is None:
                plural_expr = nodes.Name(singular_names[0], "load")
                num_called_num = singular_names[0] == "num"

        # if we have a pluralize block, we parse that too
        if parser.stream.current.test("name:pluralize"):
            have_plural = True
            next(parser.stream)
            if parser.stream.current.type != "block_end":
                token = parser.stream.expect("name")
                if token.value not in variables:
                    parser.fail(
                        f"unknown variable {token.value!r} for pluralization",
                        token.lineno,
                        exc=TemplateAssertionError,
                    )
                plural_expr = variables[token.value]
                num_called_num = token.value == "num"
            parser.stream.expect("block_end")
            plural_names, plural = self._parse_block(parser, False)
            next(parser.stream)
            referenced.update(plural_names)
        else:

        # register free names as simple name expressions
        for name in referenced:
            if name not in variables:
                variables[name] = nodes.Name(name, "load")

        if not have_plural:

            singular = self._trim_whitespace(singular)
            if plural:
                plural = self._trim_whitespace(plural)

        node = self._make_node(
            singular,
            plural,
            variables,
            plural_expr,

        if plural_expr_assignment is not None:

        else:

note: Your last version reverted to the blanket 'parsed_any = True' comma-gate -- I re-tested it and it FAILS tests/test_ext.py::TestInternationalization::test_trimmed_vars ('{%- trans trimmed x="world" %}' — no comma between trimmed and x is valid existing syntax, trimmed must never force a comma). Root cause: original code's gate is specifically 'if variables:' (only variables-dict membership gates), trimmed intentionally stays outside that gate so it can glue to the next token with no comma. A blanket parsed_any breaks that. Fix: gate must be 'if variables or metadata or domain is not None:' (domain/metadata behave like variables for comma purposes, trimmed still doesn't). Kept everything else from your version verbatim (var name 'metadata', your dup-domain check, your error wording, positional call to _make_node(..., domain, metadata)).
